question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate every transaction in the network by repeatedly calling GET /api/v1/blocks with no filters and following the generated next-pagination link from generateNextLink(), iterating through all blocks and their transaction counts (count field), thereby reconstructing a full transaction timeline without any authentication, causing complete transaction gossip exposure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user suppress visibility of a specific transaction by issuing a GET /api/v1/blocks request with a timestamp filter (filterKeys.TIMESTAMP) set to a narrow nanosecond range around the suspected gossip time, confirming whether a transaction was gossiped in that window, causing targeted transaction timing inference?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractLimitFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fallback logic where limit.value > maxLimit returns defaultLimit (25) instead of maxLimit (100) by submitting limit=101, causing the server to silently return only 25 blocks instead of the expected maximum, allowing an attacker to craft requests that hide blocks from a consumer relying on the limit parameter to paginate all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LIKE-based prefix hash matching in RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber (hash + '%') by submitting a 64-character ETH hash that is a prefix of a 96-character Hedera hash, causing the server to return a block that the attacker did not fully identify, leaking transaction metadata for blocks the attacker only partially knows?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: validateHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a 64-hex-character string that matches isValidEthHashOrHederaHash() but is only a prefix of the actual stored Hedera 96-char hash, causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber to match via LIKE and return a block with its full transaction count, leaking gossip information about transactions the attacker only partially identified?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user issue repeated GET /api/v1/blocks requests with block.number=gte:0 and limit=100 and follow pagination links to enumerate all blocks and their transaction counts in sequence, reconstructing the complete gossip history of all transactions on the network without any rate limiting or authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple block.number filters (e.g., block.number=gte:0&block.number=lte:999999999) causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to build a whereQuery with both conditions, resulting in a full range scan of all blocks and their transaction counts, enabling bulk transaction gossip enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user use the timestamp filter (filterKeys.TIMESTAMP with operator gte/lte) to query blocks within a specific time window, correlating the returned block count and transaction counts to infer when specific transactions were gossiped, causing transaction timing deanonymization?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that generateNextLink() always anchors pagination on filterKeys.BLOCK_NUMBER (last(blocks).index) regardless of whether the query was ordered by timestamp, causing the next link to skip or repeat blocks when the user was filtering by timestamp, allowing transactions to be hidden from paginated enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractOrderFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple order parameters (e.g., order=asc&order=desc) causing extractOrderFromFilters() to use findLast() and honor only the last value, while extractOrderByFromFilters() uses the first filter, creating an inconsistency between sort column and sort direction that causes transactions to appear in an unexpected order, hiding specific transactions from sequential consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user issue a GET /api/v1/blocks request with no filters, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a full table scan with no WHERE clause, and by observing the response time difference between requests, infer the total number of blocks and transactions gossiped on the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp filter with a nanosecond-precision value (e.g., timestamp=1676540001.234490000) to pinpoint the exact consensus_end of a block, confirming the precise gossip timestamp of the last transaction in that block, enabling transaction timing attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user submit block number 0 (isPositiveLong with allowZero=true) to retrieve the genesis block and its transaction count, then use that as a baseline to enumerate all subsequent blocks and transactions via pagination, causing full network transaction gossip history exposure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a block.number filter with operator eq and a specific block number to retrieve a single block's transaction count (count field in BlockViewModel), confirming whether a specific transaction was included in that block, causing targeted transaction gossip confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractLimitFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user submit limit=1 to retrieve blocks one at a time, using the pagination next link to iterate through every block individually, making it harder for rate-limiting systems to detect bulk enumeration while still reconstructing the full transaction gossip history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of utils.validateReq(req) in getBlocks() (unlike getByHashOrNumber() which calls it) to submit unexpected request parameters that bypass validation, causing unintended query behavior that leaks transaction gossip data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user submit both block.number and timestamp filters simultaneously, causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to build a whereQuery with both conditions, and since extractOrderByFromFilters() picks the first filter key, the ordering may not match the user's intent, causing transactions to be returned in an order that hides specific gossip events?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a hash prefix oracle attack by submitting progressively longer hex prefixes (starting from 1 hex char) to the /api/v1/blocks/:hashOrNumber endpoint, using the 404 vs 200 response to binary-search for a valid block hash, thereby discovering block hashes and their associated transaction counts without knowing the full hash?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user use the order=asc parameter with block.number=gte:0 to retrieve blocks in ascending order starting from genesis, and by observing the count field in each BlockViewModel, reconstruct the exact number of transactions gossiped in each consensus round, causing full transaction gossip enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp filter with operator lte and a future timestamp value, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to return all blocks up to that timestamp including recently gossiped transactions, enabling real-time transaction gossip monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the pagination next link generated by generateNextLink() by modifying the block.number anchor value in the URL, causing the server to skip or repeat blocks in the sequence, allowing specific transaction-containing blocks to be excluded from enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user issue a high-frequency series of GET /api/v1/blocks requests with timestamp=gte:{current_time} to poll for newly gossiped blocks in near-real-time, effectively monitoring the gossip stream of all new transactions as they are finalized?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractOrderByFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that extractOrderByFromFilters() picks the first filter in the array (index [0]) while extractLimitFromFilters() uses findLast(), by submitting filters in a specific order to cause the orderBy column to be consensus_end while the limit is unexpectedly defaulted, causing transactions to be returned in an order that exposes gossip timing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a block hash with mixed case (e.g., 0xFF90773...) to validateHashOrNumber(), which strips the 0x prefix but does not lowercase, while RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() lowercases it before the LIKE query, causing a case-normalization discrepancy that could be exploited to probe for blocks in a way that bypasses any case-sensitive logging or monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a block.number filter with a very large range (e.g., block.number=gte:0&block.number=lte:9223372036854775807) to cause RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a full table scan, consuming excessive database resources and causing a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate transaction gossip queries from completing?
